failure_modes.text: A negotiation can look efficient on paper while failing because positions, concessions, and credibility are interpreted differently by different audiences.
failure_modes.source_quote: The model has high leverage when the decision is not just what deal is efficient, but how positions, concessions, credibility, and sequencing will be interpreted by multiple audiences.
failure_modes.mitigation: Map how positions, concessions, and credibility are likely to be interpreted by the other side, not just how they are intended.

failure_modes.text: Coalition narratives can filter out disconfirming intelligence before it affects the negotiating posture.
failure_modes.source_quote: international-negotiation-and-diplomacy-models conflicts with confirmation-bias (confirmation bias) when coalition narratives filter out disconfirming intelligence.
failure_modes.mitigation: Check coalition narratives against disconfirming intelligence before they harden into the negotiating posture.

failure_modes.text: Technical correctness can fail to move the system if interests, legitimacy, or face have not been handled.
failure_modes.source_quote: It is especially useful when technical correctness alone will not move the system unless interests, face, legitimacy, and communication are managed together.
failure_modes.mitigation: Address interests, legitimacy, and face alongside technical correctness; correct proposals fail when relational dimensions are ignored.

premortem_questions.text: How will each relevant audience interpret this concession or sequence step differently?
premortem_questions.source_quote: positions, concessions, credibility, and sequencing will be interpreted by multiple audiences

premortem_questions.text: What politically convenient story are we accepting because it fits the coalition narrative?
premortem_questions.source_quote: coalition narratives filter out disconfirming intelligence

premortem_questions.text: What fact-grounded option are we dropping because signaling pressure makes it harder to say aloud?
premortem_questions.source_quote: political signaling pressures displace fact-grounded option analysis

heuristics.text: Model the negotiation as a multi-audience interpretation problem, not just as a single efficient bargain.
Sequence concessions with explicit attention to credibility, legitimacy, and retaliation risk.
Force disconfirming intelligence into the posture review before public narratives harden.

curation_notes.summary: Focused on credibility, audience interpretation, and coalition effects rather than on generic diplomacy language.
donor_drops: Dropped broader donor-style elaboration in favor of three compact source-grounded intervention chunks per type.
open_questions: Relation richness stays in Wave 3; Wave 2 only preserves the intervention surface needed for preview substrate use.

allies: Wave 3 preserves the first-order ally surface where the raw source gives a clean current-vocabulary seam.
antagonists: Wave 3 preserves the strongest current-vocabulary conflicts and leaves weaker cross-model seams omitted on purpose.
structured_tensions: Wave 4 remains observational only; this batch does not widen higher-order source coverage.
